workflow.name: Elasticsearch HA existing
workflow.description: Bootstrap and validate Elasticsearch HA from an existing automation runner and target inventory.

data.label: Prepare Elasticsearch inventory
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
apt-get update
DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y ansible sshpass
cat >/tmp/elasticsearch-inventory.ini <<'INVENTORY'
[elasticsearch]
10.31.95.11 role=elasticsearch name=elasticsearch-01
10.31.95.12 role=elasticsearch name=elasticsearch-02
10.31.95.13 role=elasticsearch name=elasticsearch-03
INVENTORY

data.label: Install Elasticsearch container runtime
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >/tmp/elasticsearch-runtime.yml <<'PLAYBOOK'
- hosts: elasticsearch
  become: true
  tasks:
    - ansible.builtin.shell: |
        if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          docker --version
          exit 0
        fi
        if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          apt-get update
          DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y docker.io docker-compose-plugin
        elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          dnf install -y docker docker-compose-plugin
        elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          yum install -y docker docker-compose-plugin
        fi
        systemctl enable --now docker
PLAYBOOK
ansible-playbook -i /tmp/elasticsearch-inventory.ini /tmp/elasticsearch-runtime.yml

data.label: Render Elasticsearch cluster manifests
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >/tmp/elasticsearch-deploy.yml <<'PLAYBOOK'
- hosts: elasticsearch
  become: true
  tasks:
    - ansible.builtin.file:
        path: /opt/octastack/elasticsearch
        state: directory
        mode: "0750"
    - ansible.builtin.copy:
        dest: /opt/octastack/elasticsearch/compose.yml
        mode: "0640"
        content: |
          services:
            elasticsearch:
              image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.15.0
              container_name: octastack-elasticsearch
              restart: unless-stopped
              environment:
                discovery_type: "single-node"
                xpack_security_enabled: "false"
                ES_JAVA_OPTS: "-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
              ports:
                - "9200:9200"
              volumes:
                - data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          volumes:
            data: {}
PLAYBOOK

data.label: Deploy Elasticsearch cluster
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >>/tmp/elasticsearch-deploy.yml <<'PLAYBOOK'
    - ansible.builtin.shell: docker compose -f /opt/octastack/elasticsearch/compose.yml pull
    - ansible.builtin.shell: docker compose -f /opt/octastack/elasticsearch/compose.yml up -d
    - ansible.builtin.shell: docker compose -f /opt/octastack/elasticsearch/compose.yml ps
PLAYBOOK
ansible-playbook -i /tmp/elasticsearch-inventory.ini /tmp/elasticsearch-deploy.yml

data.label: Document Elasticsearch topology
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >/tmp/elasticsearch-topology.md <<'EOF'
# Elasticsearch HA topology

Members:
- elasticsearch-01: 10.31.95.11 (elasticsearch)
- elasticsearch-02: 10.31.95.12 (elasticsearch)
- elasticsearch-03: 10.31.95.13 (elasticsearch)

This example deploys one Elasticsearch container per member host. Replace the placeholder commands with vendor-supported clustering, TLS, backup, and failover automation before production use.
EOF
cat /tmp/elasticsearch-topology.md

data.label: Validate Elasticsearch cluster
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
ansible -i /tmp/elasticsearch-inventory.ini elasticsearch -b -m shell -a 'docker ps --format "{{.Names}} {{.Status}}"'
